title: TypeScript, C# and Turbo Pascal with Anders Hejlsberg
summary: Anders Hejlsberg reflects on building Turbo Pascal, C#, and TypeScript, and shares what AI may change about the future of software engineering.

title: Revisiting “No Silver Bullets” in the age of AI
summary: Does the noted “No Silver Bullets” paper by the author of a classic engineering book still hold up, 40 years later? Is AI the long-sought single silver bullet – or has one been around for years?

title: The Pulse: Did capacity shortages turn Anthropic hostile to devs?
summary: Also: Amazon finally allows engineers to use Claude Code and Codex, Meta forcefully assigns engineers to data labelling work ahead of layoffs, more small “AI-forward” teams, and more

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications: The Cloud & Doing the Right Thing
summary: How the cloud changes the way we build applications, and why engineers’ ethical choices matter more than ever. Excerpt from the book, ‘Designing Data-Intensive Applications’, 2nd edition

title: The Pulse: AI load breaks GitHub – why not other vendors?
summary: Also: Anthropic’s speed run to break devs’ goodwill, big price increases from GitHub Copilot, Mitchell Hashimoto on the “building block economy,” and more
